article.title: Countable vs. Uncountable Nouns
article.description: Countable nouns have a singular/plural form and can follow numbers: 'one apple, two apples.' Uncountable nouns have no plural and can't follow numbers directly: 'information' (not 'informations'), 'advice', 'furniture', 'water'. Use 'much' with uncountable nouns and 'many' with countable: 'How much water?' vs 'How many apples?'
article.content: Countable nouns have a singular/plural form and can follow numbers: 'one apple, two apples.' Uncountable nouns have no plural and can't follow numbers directly: 'information' (not 'informations'), 'advice', 'furniture', 'water'. Use 'much' with uncountable nouns and 'many' with countable: 'How much water?' vs 'How many apples?'

generated.caption:
Ever wondered when to use 'much' or 'many'? 🤔 It's all about countable vs. uncountable nouns! Countable nouns have a plural (like 'apples'), but uncountable nouns don't (like 'water' or 'information'). Use 'many' for countable things, and 'much' for uncountable. For example, 'How much water?' vs. 'How many apples?' Easy, right? 😉

Save this tip and follow @TheEnglishVault for daily English lessons!
generated.script: Ye Countable aur Uncountable nouns mein confusion hota hai na? Dekho, Countable nouns wo hote hain jinhe hum count kar sakte hain. Jaise 'one apple, two apples'. Inka singular aur plural form hota hai. But Uncountable nouns ka koi plural form nahi hota. Aap 'informations' ya 'advices' nahi bol sakte. Ye 'information', 'advice', 'furniture', 'water' jaise words hain. Ab inke saath, 'much' aur 'many' ka use dekho. Uncountable nouns ke saath 'much' aata hai, jaise 'How much water?'. Aur Countable nouns ke saath 'many' aata hai, jaise 'How many apples?'. Toh 'informations' ya 'advices' jaisi galti ab mat karna. 'How much water?' aur 'How many apples?' hi sahi hai. Ab confusion clear ho gaya na? Comment mein batao, aapko ye pehle se pata tha kya? Aur follow karo The English Vault ko aise hi tips ke liye!

slides.body: In English, nouns can be classified into two main categories: countable and uncountable. Countable nouns, such as 'apple', have a singular and plural form and can be used with numbers. On the other hand, uncountable nouns like 'information', 'advice', and 'water' do not have a plural form and cannot be used directly with numbers. For instance, we say 'one apple, two apples', but we cannot say 'one water, two waters'. When asking about uncountable nouns, we use 'much', whereas for countable nouns, we use 'many', as in 'How much water?' versus 'How many apples?'. Understanding this distinction is crucial for effective communication in English.
